top_stories.title: Omada Introduces Omada Identity Sovereign, Bringing Digital Sovereignty for Regulated Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada has launched Omada Identity Sovereign, a new identity governance solution designed for regulated European organizations that allows deployment on infrastructure of their choosing with no non-European entities in the chain. The product addresses digital sovereignty requirements for regulated industries by enabling fully European-controlled IGA operations.

top_stories.title: Okta expands Cross App Access ecosystem to secure AI agent connections
top_stories.summary: Okta announced that more than 25 software vendors have joined its Cross App Access framework for securing AI agent connections to enterprise applications. The framework routes AI agent connections through a company's identity infrastructure to enforce access controls.

top_stories.title: Omada Identity Sovereign targets Europe’s growing digital sovereignty demands
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched Omada Identity Sovereign, a new solution designed to give organizations direct control over the deployment location and management of their identity governance infrastructure. The product targets European digital sovereignty requirements, addressing data residency, operational control, and related compliance demands.

top_stories.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
top_stories.summary: Service desks are being targeted by social engineering attacks aimed at obtaining password resets, MFA changes, and corporate account access. Specops Software provides an analysis of how these attacks operate and outlines defensive measures organizations can implement.

top_stories.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag gives AI agents independent identities
top_stories.summary: Anthropic introduced an agent identity model for Claude Tag, giving the AI assistant its own independent identity, permissions, and tool access configured by administrators within shared workspaces. The model separates Claude's access from individual user credentials, meaning the AI operates under its own identity rather than inheriting or relying on employees' personal accounts.

news.title: What your next cyber insurance renewal will demand
news.summary: A Help Net Security video features Tide Foundation co-founder Michael Loewy discussing how cyber insurers are shifting from self-attestation to verified evidence of security controls at renewal. Loewy specifically calls out MFA as an example where proof of implementation beats paperwork declarations.

news.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
news.summary: A coordinated law enforcement operation involving Europol and private sector partners including Microsoft disrupted the Amadey and StealC malware infrastructure. The operation recovered 27 million stolen credentials, targeting criminal networks used to facilitate ransomware, financial fraud, and attacks on critical infrastructure.

news.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
news.summary: Attackers breached application vendor Klue and used stolen OAuth tokens to access and exfiltrate Salesforce customer data, with the scope of victims expanding. The attack vector involves compromised OAuth tokens, highlighting risks in federated authorization and third-party application access chains.

news.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
news.summary: Threat actors exploited FortiGate firewalls using a Golang-based credential sniffer in an ongoing global campaign. The attack targeted approximately 430,000 FortiGate devices and resulted in the identification of 110 million credentials.

news.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
news.summary: A Russian-speaking initial access broker conducted a credential-harvesting operation called FortiBleed targeting over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally, resulting in 110 million compromised credentials. The campaign, active since February 2026, involved credential list collection, exposed service enumeration, brute-forcing, and custom tooling deployment against Fortinet infrastructure.

news.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
news.summary: President Trump signed Executive Order 14409 on June 22, requiring federal agencies to migrate high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography by December 31, 2030, with digital signatures following by December 31, 2031. National security systems are placed on a separate migration track under the order.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and gain unauthorized access to corporate systems.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm has announced an investigation into claims on behalf of SailPoint investors trading under the NASDAQ ticker SAIL. The notice advises investors to contact the firm but does not detail the specific nature of the claims being investigated.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Denodo connector in Power BI (Preview)
news.summary: Microsoft has released a preview feature enabling Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Denodo connector in Power BI. When configured, users querying semantic models in DirectQuery mode are authenticated to Denodo using their own Microsoft Entra ID identity, removing on-premises dependencies.

news.title: Mastering Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: A book titled "Mastering Microsoft Entra ID" is being published on June 26, 2026, covering design, implementation, security, and governance of identity solutions in hybrid enterprise environments. The 678-page paperback is published by Packt and focuses on Microsoft Entra ID as an IAM platform.

news.title: The cybersecurity industry built a $200B business selling you problems. Nobody got paid to fix them.
news.summary: An opinion piece argues that the cybersecurity industry has prioritized risk detection over remediation, creating a $200B market built around identifying problems like dormant user accounts and excessive privileges rather than fixing them. The article touches on IAM-relevant issues such as privilege management and dormant accounts but frames them within a broader industry critique.

news.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
news.summary: The article covers SIM swap attacks as a method for bypassing SMS-based one-time password authentication, leading to account takeovers. The attack vector highlights weaknesses in SMS-based MFA, where threat actors port a victim's phone number to a device they control to intercept OTP codes.

news.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
news.summary: A 29-year-old heap over-read vulnerability in the Squid web proxy, dubbed "Squidbleed," can expose cleartext HTTP requests including credentials and session tokens to other users sharing the same proxy. The flaw originates from a 1997 FTP-parsing change and remains present in Squid's default configuration, with disclosure by Calif.io researchers in June.

news.title: Microsoft to retire Azure DevOps issuer for workload identity federation
news.summary: Microsoft has announced the deprecation of the Azure DevOps issuer for workload identity federation service connections, with a retirement date of July 1, 2027. The change is part of a broader initiative to standardize on Microsoft Entra as the issuer for workload identity federation.

news.title: Navigating the AI access control minefield
news.summary: The article addresses the challenge of managing AI systems' access to enterprise data, framing it as an emerging access control problem for IT leaders. The discussion centers on applying secure access principles to AI deployments, intersecting with authorization and access management disciplines.

news.title: 生成AI型AI-OCR「GenOCR」、SAML 認証および Microsoft Entra ID による SSO に対応 ― ユーザーのセキュリティ強化と利便性向上へ
news.summary: GenOCR, a generative AI-based OCR product by Jinbei, has added support for SAML authentication and SSO via Microsoft Entra ID. The update is aimed at improving security and convenience for users of the OCR platform.

news.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
news.summary: A GitHub token leak at Novo Nordisk exposed vulnerabilities in software development pipeline security, specifically around secrets management. The incident illustrates how organizations misclassify secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity and access management problem.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems exposed World Cup streaming infrastructure to potential remote takeover due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The flaw allowed unauthorized access that could have enabled a malicious actor to hijack live broadcasts, highlighting misconfiguration risks in Entra deployments.

news.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
news.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting attack has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries. Attackers have compiled a list of working credentials from the compromised devices, actively targeting multiple sectors globally.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including high-profile ones belonging to the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot into performing unauthorized password resets were shared on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, causing a credential leak that CISA is still working to contain. Congressional lawmakers from both chambers are demanding answers from CISA as the agency works to invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials for highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems in a public GitHub repository. The leaked files contained details about CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
